name: A J Gallacher
registrationNotes: Although named 'A J Gallacher', this tartan is not a restricted personal tartan. Anyone with the surname Gallacher may wear it. It is a warp pre-aligned design based on the 1280 heavyweight wool thread limit for single width warp, and weft on a 'Narrow Fast' shuttle loom. Warping and kiltmaking directions were submitted with the application for registration and are available from the Scottish Register of Tartans on request.

name: A Man's a Man St Petersburg
restrictions: Yes. Please contact Robert Burns World Federation regarding use/weaving/reproduction of the tartan.
registrationNotes: This tartan was commissioned by the Robert Burns World Federation. The tartan was derived from an International Essay Competition created by The Robert Burns World Federation, in which the Russian Children were asked to explain in their own words what they felt Burns’ famous Poem ‘A Man’s A Man for A’ That’ was trying to say. Their answer was global brother and sisterhood. The question was posed: if the poem could be represented in a tartan what would the tartan look like? This design was the outcome and it is intended to be a symbol of belonging, unity and inclusiveness.

name: A Man's a Man Ukraine
restrictions: Yes. Please contact Robert Burns World Federation regarding use/weaving/reproduction of the tartan.
registrationNotes: This tartan was commissioned by the Robert Burns World Federation. name: A Thread in Time
restrictions: Yes. Intended for the exclusive use of the direct descendants of Trisha L. and J. Christopher Marmo, along with their spouses (for as long as they are married); immediate family (siblings, nieces/nephews, aunts/uncles) or with special permission by Trisha L. Marmo and her heirs. Permission to weave must be sought from Trisha L Marmo & her heirs and Araminta Campbell.
registrationNotes: This tartan was designed to celebrate the book ‘A Thread in Time’, written by Trisha Marmo, which captures the essence of love, Scottish heritage, and the shared threads of family woven across time. It was commissioned by the author and her husband to on the couple’s 30th wedding anniversary in 2024. The dark blue, composed of 30 threads, is intended to symbolise their three decades together since their wedding on April 24th, 1994 and the blue are grey checks, made of 24 threads, are to honour the 24th day. The couple’s five children are intended to be represented in the tartan in the red and white checks. The tartan’s was inspired by Trisha’s recently discovered Scottish heritage, rooted in the Skene and Forbes Clans.

name: A2 (Personal)
registrationDate: This tartan was recorded prior to the launch of The Scottish Register of Tartans.
registrationNotes: Designed by Albert van Kampen of The Netherlands using the colours of the Dutch flag and blue to represent the water that the Dutch 'fight for and against'. The name A2 was chosen because both Albert & his wife's first names begin with A.

name: Aaltonen, Roope (Personal)
restrictions: Yes. For the exclusive use of Roope Aaltonen and his descendants (in current and all future generations) and immediate family (spouse, parents, siblings), and anyone else expressly authorised by Roope Aaltonen.
registrationNotes: A personal tartan for the use of Roope Aaltonen, his family and descendants.

name: Loch Lomond Whisky
restrictions: Yes. Exclusively designed by Kinloch Anderson for Loch Lomond Whisky. Restricted availability, please contact Kinloch Anderson regarding use. May only to be woven with permission from Kinloch Anderson Ltd.
registrationNotes: This company tartan was designed exclusively for Loch Lomond Whisky. Inspiration was taken from elements of the Colquhoun tartan as the Colquhoun Clan has associations with the Loch Lomond area, Inchmoan and Dunbartonshire. The threadcount includes the number ten to reference the brand’s ten-year anniversary. The design uses the Loch Lomond Whisky brand colour palette.
